hawkish_phrases: a small degree of excess demand emerges over the second half of the forecast period
inflation is projected to be 2% in two years' time
rising quite sharply towards the target
dovish_phrases: a further easing of monetary policy is warranted
increase the target stock of purchased UK government bonds by an additional £150 billion
If the outlook for inflation weakens, the Committee stands ready to take whatever additional action is necessary
The Committee does not intend to tighten monetary policy at least until there is clear evidence that significant progress is being made in eliminating spare capacity
the risks around the GDP projection are judged to be skewed to the downside
summary: The MPC voted unanimously to hold Bank Rate at 0.1% and increase asset purchases by £150 billion to £875 billion total, with explicit forward guidance stating readiness for additional easing and no tightening until spare capacity is eliminated and 2% inflation achieved sustainably.
